description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone fragment with a weathered, uneven surface and a distinct carved relief on one side. The front face features a stylized animal head, possibly a fox or wolf, with a prominent snout and a single visible eye, while the top edge is adorned with a series of shallow, parallel grooves. The reverse side is flatter and less detailed, showing natural stone texture and some discoloration. One side bears what appears to be an inscription or decorative motif, partially eroded but still visible. The stone's coloration ranges from pale beige to light brown, with some darker patches indicating age and exposure. Its overall form suggests it may have once been part of a larger architectural or sculptural element, now serving as an obstacle or marker in an urban setting.
description: A weathered, rectangular stone fragment with a carved animal head relief and shallow grooves, featuring an inscription on one side and natural discoloration throughout.
description_view_0: The front face shows a flat, weathered surface with faint discoloration and a partially visible inscription along the right edge.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the stone's thickness, ribbed grooves along the top, and the rough, uneven texture of the surface.
description_view_2: The main relief is visible here, depicting a stylized animal head with a pronounced snout, single eye, and decorative grooves at the top.
description_view_3: This edge view emphasizes the stone's depth, the inscription running vertically, and the irregular, chipped lower edge.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or marker on the ground
attribute_pattern: Carved relief of an animal head with parallel grooves and a partially eroded inscription; irregular natural mottling and discoloration.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible erosion, surface discoloration, and softened carved details indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces or manicured lawns, often standing alone or partially surrounded by grass or gravel, drawing attention as a sculptural or historical element.
